title: docs(perf): reclassify audit finding N1 as time-bounded current-bottleneck refutation
intent: Corrects PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md: finding N1 (JSON art-jobs store, 22ms/parse) was over-classified as a confirmed bottleneck (#9). The pre-migration store was dormant on 2026-07-04 — 43 records, all created in a 32-day window (Jan-Feb 2026), no writes in ~5 months — so by the same tiny-n logic that refuted leads #1/#2 it was not a current bottleneck. Reclassify to a time-bounded current-bottleneck refutation while preserving that the old full-file parse/rewrite design was structurally inefficient and still justified correctness/maintenance cleanup.

diff_articulation.what_changed: N1 reclassified VERIFIED/#9 -> REFUTED AS A CURRENT BOTTLENECK with the dormancy evidence (43 records, 32-day creation window, ~5 months idle); added to the §2 refuted table; struck from the §0 summary table and the §5 one-hour perf fix list; restores a time-bounded §4/§6 frontier note for pre-#189 or reverted JSON paths; records that PR #189 has now merged as correctness/maintenance cleanup, not proof that N1 was an observed perf bottleneck.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Documentation-only correction of a mis-ranked finding; aligns the audit's classification with its own tiny-n refutation logic without erasing the structural inefficiency or turning a point-in-time dormancy observation into a permanent guarantee.

commands_run: grep review: no remaining reference treats N1/#9 as a confirmed current bottleneck; §0/§2/§3/§4/§5/§6 distinguish current perf refutation from structural cleanup
PR #189 state checked via gh: MERGED 2026-07-04; audit updated from 'open at time of writing' to merged correctness/maintenance cleanup

files.intent: Reclassify finding N1 (art-jobs store) as a time-bounded current-bottleneck refutation while preserving the structural-maintenance concern.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Docs-only classification correction; no code or runtime change. The revised wording explicitly preserves the old structural inefficiency, records PR #189 as merged cleanup, and keeps the dormancy conclusion time-bounded to the 2026-07-04 audit evidence.
